load_bearing_rule: Weak directional compatibility is not load-bearing unless the matched R1a-comparator envelope is also entered and positive controls pass
figure_claim_allowed: Figure may show that mask flexibility can create weak directional compatibility
claim_boundary: Not evidence of PH908 geographic origin, ethnic identity, population continuity, chronology, or migration route

analysis_unit: PH908 state compared with within-state R-Z280/R-M458 threshold
observed_result: 0/10 PH908 states enter the matched state-specific R1a-comparator envelope
load_bearing_rule: Matched-envelope entry is required for any load-bearing adversarial interpretation
figure_claim_allowed: Figure may state that the strict comparator envelope remains closed
claim_boundary: Does not identify PH908 origin and does not convert comparator behavior into PH908 history

analysis_unit: Matched R1a comparator recovery under the same state definition
observed_result: 6/10 states recover both positive controls and 4/10 are transparency-only control-failing states
load_bearing_rule: Control-failing states are retained for transparency but cannot support load-bearing adversarial interpretation
figure_claim_allowed: Figure may separate positive-control-passing states from transparency-only states
claim_boundary: Positive-control recovery calibrates the stress test and does not establish PH908 origin, continuity, or route

analysis_unit: PH908 source-adequacy multiplier still needed to reach the matched R1a threshold
observed_result: 10/10 states still require multiplier greater than 1; observed range 1.962688 to 5.634028
load_bearing_rule: Visibility burden is an arithmetic sensitivity calculation and not a bias correction or population-size estimate
figure_claim_allowed: Figure may state that additional source adequacy would still be needed to reach the comparator threshold
claim_boundary: Not a public-tree bias correction, not a population-size estimate, and not origin evidence

analysis_unit: Scoreable boundary-by-rarefaction cells across source layers, comparators, contexts, and equal-n settings
observed_result: 376/544 cells remain positive and 168/544 fail in the full grid
load_bearing_rule: Full-grid failures must be interpreted as adequacy or denominator limits unless a stronger predefined test is supplied
figure_claim_allowed: Figure may show that the full rarefaction grid is not uniformly positive
claim_boundary: Not a universal all-grid pass and not evidence of geographic origin, ethnic identity, continuity, or route

analysis_unit: Scoreable cells at higher common-n thresholds
denominator_definition: 408 scoreable cells at n >= 30 and 272 scoreable cells at n >= 50
observed_result: 373/408 cells remain positive at n >= 30 and 272/272 remain positive at n >= 50
load_bearing_rule: High-common-n summaries support an adequacy interpretation of low-common-n failures but are not universal decision gates
figure_claim_allowed: Figure may state that low-common-n failures disappear at n >= 50 in this trace
claim_boundary: Common-n thresholds are adequacy diagnostics not origin evidence and not universal validation claims

analysis_unit: Matched R1a comparator states under source-terminal thinning, source-country thinning, and audited branch-layer collapse
observed_result: 30/72 states remain basic compatible; at retained source visibility 100 percent and 50 percent the controls retain 10/12 basic-compatible branch-layer states
load_bearing_rule: Compatibility is recomputed from synthetic A_s, branch-layer D, and country-visibility gates; matched R1a-comparator compatibility is stricter than basic compatibility
figure_claim_allowed: Figure may describe Panel D as synthetic visibility-degradation controls for matched R1a positive comparators
claim_boundary: Synthetic degradation is an operating-characteristic stress test and not a population-size estimate or origin claim
